title: Anyone can increase liquidity on any Uniswap V3 position
reason: By design: the caller only contributes their own assets to someone else's position and cannot extract value or create protocol-level harm.

title: Anyone can mint a new position with any parameters
reason: By design: public minting uses the caller's own funds and does not move or lock third-party assets.

title: Unchecked swap when amountIn is zero
reason: `_swap` is gated by `amountIn > 0`, so zero-input `swapData` is inert. The real issue is arbitrary external execution during non-zero swaps, captured in F-002.

title: No deadline validation in execute callback
reason: No standalone exploit: the price-sensitive Uniswap subcalls already receive `deadline`, and stale execution only becomes harmful through the authorization flaw captured in F-001.

title: No validation of recipient address
reason: Using `address(0)` only burns funds chosen by the caller or by an attacker already covered by F-001; it is not an independent vulnerability.

title: Missing slippage protection in changeRange new position mint
reason: `CHANGE_RANGE` does mint with zero mins, but leftovers are returned; absent another bug, this is strategy quality/user choice rather than realistic protocol-level harm.

title: No access control on swap function
reason: Public swap functionality is intentional; callers expose only their own assets and chosen routing data.

title: Missing event for swapAndIncreaseLiquidity input validation failure
reason: Operational visibility only; not a security issue.
